_meta.description: Historical crisis episodes used to backtest the risk engine. Every event is a real, publicly documented incident with its actual date. The backtest replays these events through the SAME Bayesian engine used live, then compares the risk trajectory against actual Brent prices for the window.
_meta.method: Events are fed to the engine on their historical dates. Lead time = days between the engine's risk score crossing the alert threshold and the episode's peak price impact date.
sources: Widely reported incidents (Reuters/AP/BBC coverage 2019-2024); dates are matters of public record

episodes.name: Gulf Tanker Crisis → Abqaiq Attack (2019)
episodes.peak_impact_note: Abqaiq-Khurais drone attack knocked out ~5.7 mb/d of Saudi processing; Brent jumped ~15% on 16 Sep 2019 — largest single-day rise since 1991.

events.summary: US ends Iran oil sanction waivers; Iran threatens to close Strait of Hormuz
Four tankers sabotaged off Fujairah anchorage (limpet mines)
Houthi drones strike Saudi East-West pipeline pump stations
Front Altair and Kokuka Courageous attacked in Gulf of Oman
Iran shoots down US RQ-4 surveillance drone over Strait of Hormuz
IRGC seizes British-flagged tanker Stena Impero in Hormuz
Drone/cruise-missile attack on Abqaiq processing facility and Khurais field

episodes.name: Red Sea Shipping Crisis (2023-24)
episodes.peak_impact_note: By 18 Dec 2023, Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC, CMA CGM and BP had suspended Red Sea transits; mass rerouting via Cape of Good Hope added 10-14 days and spiked freight/war-risk premiums.

events.summary: USS Carney intercepts Houthi missiles/drones over southern Red Sea
Houthis hijack car carrier Galaxy Leader in southern Red Sea
Three commercial vessels attacked in Bab el-Mandeb approaches
Tanker Strinda struck by Houthi cruise missile near Bab el-Mandeb
Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd suspend all Red Sea transits after Al Jasrah hit
BP pauses all Red Sea oil shipments; major carriers reroute via Cape
US/UK strikes on Houthi targets; Houthis vow to widen attacks on shipping

episodes.name: Iran-Israel Direct Escalation (Apr 2024)
episodes.peak_impact_note: IRGC seized MSC Aries near Hormuz on 13 Apr; Iran launched direct missile/drone attack on Israel 13-14 Apr. Brent had risen ~10% from mid-March into the mid-$90s on escalation risk before de-escalating.

events.summary: Strike on Iranian consulate in Damascus kills IRGC commanders; Iran vows retaliation
Iran threatens to close Strait of Hormuz amid retaliation warnings
IRGC commandos seize MSC Aries container ship near Strait of Hormuz
Iran launches 300+ drones/missiles at Israel in first direct attack
Reported Israeli strike near Isfahan; regional shipping on high alert

episodes.name: Ever Given Grounding (Mar 2021)
episodes.peak_impact_note: Ultra-large container ship Ever Given ran aground diagonally across the canal in high winds, fully blocking transit for six days; roughly 370 vessels queued at both ends before it was refloated 29 Mar. Deliberately included as a contrast case: this was a sudden accident, not an escalating geopolitical crisis, so there is no rhetoric or incident buildup for the engine to have caught in advance. A near-zero lead time here is the model working correctly, not a failure of it.

events.summary: Container ship Ever Given runs aground, blocking Suez Canal in both directions
Canal remains fully blocked; roughly 370 vessels queued, salvage efforts ongoing

episodes.name: Hormuz Sanctions Standoff (2011-12)
episodes.peak_impact_note: No physical attack occurred in this episode: Iran repeatedly threatened to close the strait in response to the incoming EU oil embargo, backed by naval exercises. Brent rose into the high $120s by March 2012 on the standoff. Included as the rhetoric-only counterpart to 2019/2024: it tests whether the engine can price sustained threat activity correctly without over-reacting to words alone.

events.summary: Iranian vice president threatens to close Strait of Hormuz if oil sanctions are imposed
Iranian navy commander repeats Hormuz closure threat during Velayat-90 naval exercises
EU formally agrees to an oil embargo on Iran; Iranian officials renew closure warnings
Iran conducts further naval drills near Hormuz amid heightened regional tension
